content.problemStatement:
Our little chef is fond of doing additions/sums in his free time. Today, he has an array **A** consisting of **N** positive integers and he will compute prefix and suffix sums over this array.

He first defines two functions **prefixSum(i)** and **suffixSum(i)** for the array as follows. The function **prefixSum(i)** denotes the sum of first **i** numbers of the array. Similarly, he defines **suffixSum(i)** as the sum of last **N - i + 1** numbers of the array.

Little Chef is interested in finding the minimum index **i** for which the value **prefixSum(i) + suffixSum(i)** is the minimum. In other words, first you should minimize the value of **prefixSum(i) + suffixSum(i)**, and then find the least index **i** for which this value is attained.

Since, he is very busy preparing the dishes for the guests, can you help him solve this problem?
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N denoting the number of integers in the array A. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, ..., AN denoting the array A.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the minimum index i for which the value prefixSum(i) + suffixSum(i) is the minimum.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The prefixSum(0) + suffixSum(0) = 1 + 10 = 11, prefixSum(1) + suffixSum(1) = 3 + 9 = 12, prefixSum(2) + suffixSum(2) = 6 + 7 = 13, prefixSum(3) + suffixSum(3) = 10 + 4 = 14. The minimum sum is 11 at index 0.

The prefixSum(0) + suffixSum(0) = 1 + 15 = 16, prefixSum(1) + suffixSum(1) = 3 + 12 = 15, prefixSum(2) + suffixSum(2) = 6 + 9 = 15, prefixSum(3) + suffixSum(3) = 10 + 5 = 15, prefixSum(4) + suffixSum(4) = 15 + 0 = 15. The minimum sum is 15 at index 1, 2, 3, or 4.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can iterate over each index i in the array A and calculate the prefixSum(i) and suffixSum(i). We keep track of the minimum sum and the corresponding index. The time complexity of this approach is O(T*N) where T is the number of test cases and N is the maximum size of the array A.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T*N) — per test case is O(N), total is O(T*N)
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we only use a constant amount of space to store the minimum sum and the corresponding index

code.Python3:
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(input())
    A = list(map(int, input().split()))
    minSum = float('inf')
    minIndex = -1
    for i in range(N):
        prefixSum = sum(A[:i+1])
        suffixSum = sum(A[i:])
        if prefixSum + suffixSum < minSum:
            minSum = prefixSum + suffixSum
            minIndex = i
    print(minIndex + 1)
